content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcer- PRACTTcEs CovrurssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322_0886
February 75,2007
Kathern A. Bhardwaj
Trinidad City Council
P OBox792
Trinidad, California 9 557 0
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-07-0ll
Dear Ms. Bhardwaj:
This letter is in_response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of
interest provisions of the political Reform Act (the ,,Act)-i Q--- --..o
QUESTION
As councilmembers with the city of Trinidad, may you and Mr. Stan Binnie
participate in decisions_involving the cellular site on Trinidad Head, or about cellular
facilities that might be located in other areas of yo ur citfl
CONCLUSION
Based on the facts that you have provided, you have no economic interest in the
current decisions involving the cellular sit" o, Trinidad Head, therefore the Act,s conflict
of interest provisions do_ not preclude you from participating in these decisions. with
regatd to decisions involving other cellular faciities that might be located in other areas
of your city, we do not have sufficient information to make a determination.
FACTS
Both you and |rr. Stan Binnie (who has given you authori zationto request advice
on his behalf) are newly elected members of the Trinidad city council. you were seated
on the council in December 2006. Before the election both of you were active in a
citizens'goup known as Friends of Trinidad Head ("Friends"; that opposed a cell tower
development by u.s. cellular ("usc") on city-owned property on Trinidad Head.
' Go-'emm"t't Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2,sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
ffi


File No. A-07-0tt
Page No. 2
Both you and Mr. Binnie frequently wrote or spoke to the city council and
planning commission during the various stages of the Lsc application. In addition, you
were also quoted in a few newspaper articles and wrote leuers to the editors of local
newspapers.
After you and Mr. Binnie wer.g e_l_ectgd, both of you resigned in writing from
Friends' However, Mr. Binnies's, wife^Kimberly tays"ninnie, i-s still active in the group
You stated in ourphone conversation of February 6,io07,that Mrs. Binnie only
volunteers with Friends and does not receive *y rui*y or other financial benefit from
the group.
Ye'nzon Wireless has approached the city (although no permits have yet been
filed) with plans to enlarge the fenced areathat"o.rtuir. the tower site, enlarge the
existing equipment shed, and install a generator with a 2|}-gallondiesel fuel tank on the
site.
In our conversation of February 6,2006, you stated that neither you nor
Mr' Binnie have any economic interests- for instance, real property ini*ri. within 500
feet of the cell tower site, or any business interests-involved in tfrese decisions.
You wantto know whether you and Mr. Binnie must recuse yourselves from these
city council decisions regarding theVerizon Wireless proposal for the cellular site on
Trinidad Head, or about cellular facilities that might ue toiated in other areas of your
ctty.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict_-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will ..perform
their duties in an impartial manner, frie from bias caused by ttreir o*, trur"ial interests
or the financial interests of persons who have supported them.,, ($ g1001(b).) Section
87100 prohibits anypublic official from making, participating ln'maklng, o, ott "rwis"
using his or her official position to influenc" u go'v"**ental decision in-which the
official has a financial interest.
sections 18700 through 18709 of the Commission's regulations set forth an eight-
step analysis in determining whether a public official has a conflict-of-interest in a
government decision he or she will be making.
Step 1: Are you a ,.public officialr'?
The conflict_of interest prohibition only applies to public officials. (Section
87100') As council members in the City of Triniua, both you and Mr. Binnie arepublic


File No. 4-07-011
Page No. 3
officials subject to the conflict of interest provisions of the Act.2 (Sec tion g204g;
regulation 18701(a).)
step 2: Are you making, participating in making, or influencing a
governmental decision?
The conflict of interest prohibition covers specific conduct: making, participating
in making, or attempting to use one's official position to influence a govefrmental
decision. (Section s7100.) A public official "makes a govemmental decision,, when the
official, acting within the authority of his or her office oir position, votes on a matter,
obligates or commits his or her agency to any course of aition, deiermines not to act
because of a conflict or enters into any contracfual agreement on behalf of his or her
agency. (See regulation 18702.1.)
As councilmembers, both you and Mr. Binnie will make governmental decisions
if either of you vote or participate in a decision regarding the cellular site on Trinidad
Head, or about cellular facilities that might be located in other areas of your city.
In addition to actually voting on these matters, if either of you engage in
negotiations without significant substantive review, provide advicl o.rnitJ
recofllmendations, you will be "participating" in a decision (regulation 1g702.2). you
will also be "influencing" that decision if you appear before or otherwise attempt to
influence any member, officer, emproyee or consultant of your agency. (Regulation
18702.3.)
Step 3: What are your economic interests? What are the possible sources of
a financial conflict-of-interest?
The Act's conflict of interest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from economic interests. These economic interests are aescribea in sections 1g703.1
through 18703.5 of the Commission,s regulations.
You stated in your request for advice as well as in our phone conversation of
February 6,2007, that neither you nor Mr. Binnie have any economic interests involved
in decisions regarding the Trindad Head cellular site. The acts of participating in Friends
and opposing a cell tower development by USC, as well as speaking to n"*.f,up",
2 Recusal Re.Quirements: If a public official is enumerated in section 87200 (including city
council members) and he or she has a conflict of interest in a decision noticed at a public meeting, then he
or she must: (1) immediatelyprior to the discussion of the item, orally identifr 
"u"i, typ" oi""orro-i"
interest involved in the decision as well as details ofthe economic interest, as-discuss-ei in regulation
18702'5(b), on the record of the meeting; (2) recuse himself or herself; and (3) leave the roori for the
duration ofthe discussion andlor vote on the item. For closed sessions, corsent calendars, absences and
speaking as a member of the public regarding personal interests, special rules found l" r"guiutlor, 1g702.5,
subdivisions (c) and (d) apply. (Section g7105.)


File No. 4-07-011
Page No. 4
reporters and writing letters to the editor about this topic, do not of themselves involve
any economic interest and therefore do not affect your ability to participate in decisions
regarding the project under the Act.
As to future decisions before the city council regarding other cellular facilities,
which maybe located in other areas of your city, each Jecisioi must be analyzed in terms
of the six kinds of economic interests from which conflicts of interest can arise:
1. Business Investment. You have an economic interest in a business entity in
which you, your spouse, your registered domestic parfirer, or your dependent children or
anyone acting on yourbehalfhas invested $ 2,000 ormore. (dection Alrcl6l,regulation
18703.1(a).)
2' Business Employment or Managernent. You have an economic interest in a
business entity for which you are a director, offico, partner, trustee, employee, or hold
any position of management. (section g7 1 03(d), regulation I g703. i O))
3. Real Property- You have an economic interest in real property in which you,
your spouse, your registered domestic parher, or your dependent "fritar* or anyone
acting on your behalf has invested $2,000 or more; o. r,.r"h person has a leasehold interest
in real estate. (Section 87103O), regulation 19703.2.)
4. Sources of Income. You have an economic interest in anyone, whether an
individual or an organization, from whom you have received (or from whom you have
been promised) $500 or more in income within 12 months prior to the decision about
which you are concerned. (section 87103(c), regulation rszo:.:.)
5. Gifts- You have an economic interest in anyone, whether an individual or an
organizationo who has given you gifts which total $390 or more within 12 months prior to
the decision about which you are concerned. (Section 87103(e), regulation 1g703.4.)
6. Personal Financial Effect. You have an economic interest in your personal
expenses, income, assets, or liabilities, as well as those of your immediate family. If these
expenses, income, assets or liabilities are likely to go up oi do*, by $250 or more in a
l2-monthperiod as a result of the governmental aelsion, then the iecision has a
personal financial effect on you. (section gTl}3,regulation 1g703.5.)
If, after applyng the foregoing factors to each governmental decision, you
conclude that you have no economic interest that would be affected by the dec-ision, you
ar! free to make, participate in making and otherwise use your officiaiposition to
influence that governmental decision.
If however, aftet appllng the factors, you determine that you have an economic
interest that would be affected by the decision, you should compleie the eight-step
analysis to determine if you have a financial interest that amounts to a conflict of interest


File No. A-07-011
Page No. 5
under the Act. The steps are set forth in the enclosed booklet entitled ,,CanlVote?
conflicts of Interest overview." If steps one through six apply, you have a financial
interest in a decision unress any exceptions are uppii""ui" iri.pri"r**J""no
Because we have no specific information about the decisions that will come
before you or your economiclnterests in relation to fufure decisions, we cannot advise
you as to whether or not you will have a conflict-of-interest.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
ERjsl
